Title: EOD Unit Conducts Three Disposal Operations Across Luzon in February
Location: Labo, Camarines Norte; Milagros, Masbate; Crow Valley, Capas, Tarlac
Barangay: Labo, Camarines Norte; Milagros, Masbate; Crow Valley, Capas, Tarlac
Description: Troops from the 1st Explosive Ordnance Disposal Company (1EODC) neutralized more than 800 explosive items across three operations in February 2026. On 1 February, the unit destroyed approximately 500 assorted captured, confiscated, surrendered, and recovered improvised explosive devices (IEDs) and unexploded ordnance (UXO) in Labo, Camarines Norte. On 7 February, the 3rd EOD Platoon safely neutralized 234 explosive items including improvised mines and grenades, high-explosive cartridges, rifle grenades, and other explosive accessories in Milagros, Masbate. On 18 February, the unit disposed of 134 UXO, including a 100-pound general purpose bomb and other high-explosive munitions, at Crow Valley in Capas, Tarlac.
Weapons: - Approximately 868 assorted IEDs and UXO, including improvised mines, grenades, projected grenades, rifle grenades, and 1 100-pound general purpose bomb [Images showed a Yugoslav/Serbian 81mm M72 mortar round with NR-2117 fuze, 1 40mm projected grenade, and various improvised explosive devices and explosive materials]
Actors: Military (1st Explosive Ordnance Disposal Company, 3rd EOD Platoon)

Title: DI Members Surrender F-1 Grenades, RPG, SMGs to Troops in Lanao del Sur
Location: Barangay Matalin, Malabang, Lanao del Sur
Description:
Eight former Dawlah Islamiya members surrendered an RPG launcher, three KG-9 submachine guns, three M1 Garand rifles, 80 hand grenades, and two rounds of RPG munitions to troops in Barangay Matalin, Malabang, Lanao del Sur on 7 February 2026. The former militants reportedly admitted to extorting vehicle owners and businesses on behalf of their commanders, threatening to use improvised explosive devices if the owners did not cooperate.

The surrender was facilitated through sustained community engagement and intensified security operations conducted by the 101st Infantry Brigade and the 64th Infantry Battalion, with support from local government officials including Lanao del Sur Governor Mamintal Adiong Jr. The former militants are expected to undergo reintegration through the Western Mindanao Command's reconciliation program for violent religious extremists.

Images of the weapons showed three US-origin Intratec TEC-KG9 semi-automatic submachine guns with factory markings clearly visible.
Weapons:
- 1 craft RPG-3-style launcher
- 3 KG-9 submachine guns
- 3 M1 Garand rifles
- 79 F-1 hand grenades
- 1 unidentified hand grenade
- 2 improvised PG-2-style munitions
- 24 rounds of ammunition
Actors:
NSAG (Dawlah Islamiya)
Military (101st Infantry Brigade, 64th Infantry Battalion)
Police (PNP)

Title: Police recover submachine gun and improvised explosive devices from NPA arms cache in Agusan del Sur
Location: Barangay Sta. Cruz, Rosario, Agusan del Sur
Description: Personnel from the 1st Special Action Battalion, Force Support Battalion, FIID, and 2nd Provincial Mobile Force Company, in coordination with the Agusan del Sur PPO, located and seized a suspected NPA arms cache in Barangay Sta. Cruz, Rosario, Agusan del Sur between 14 and 17 February 2026. Despite initial police reports describing two hand grenades, one IED, and 30 metres of detonating wire, images of the recovered items showed one M3 submachine gun, one craft shotgun, and several improvised explosive devices, with no grenades visible. The recovered explosives and related materials were placed under the custody of the Provincial EOD and Canine Unit of the Agusan del Sur PPO.
Weapons:
- 1 M3 submachine gun
- 1 craft shotgun
- several improvised explosive devices
- 30 metres of detonating wire
- 5 magazines for caliber 7.62mm firearms
Actors:
NSAG (CPP-NPA)
Police (1st Special Action Battalion, Force Support Battalion, FIID, 2nd Provincial Mobile Force Company, Agusan del Sur PPO, Provincial EOD and Canine Unit)

Title: Police seize M14 rifle and pistols from alleged arms trafficker in Marawi City
Location: Barangay Matampay, Marawi City, Lanao del Sur
Description: Police from the CIDG Cotabato City Field Unit and CIDG RFU-Bangsamoro, supported by the PNP Special Action Force, arrested a 56-year-old male suspect identified by the alias "Mario" in Barangay Matampay, Marawi City, Lanao del Sur on 16 February 2026. The suspect, a resident of Barangay Palao, Bobong, was apprehended while allegedly selling three unregistered firearms — one M14 rifle, one caliber .45 pistol, and one caliber .40 pistol — along with magazines and approximately 30 rounds of ammunition. The suspect is alleged to have been involved in gunrunning activities in and around Marawi City. Around the same period, police publicized a series of simultaneous operations in Negros Occidental also targeting alleged gunrunners, resulting in seven additional firearms seized.
Weapons:
- 1 M14 rifle
- 1 caliber .45 pistol
- 1 caliber .40 pistol
- approximately 30 rounds of ammunition
Actors:
Criminal individual (arms trafficker)
Police (CIDG Cotabato City Field Unit, CIDG RFU-Bangsamoro, PNP Special Action Force)

Title: NPA member surrenders submachine gun and rifle grenades to state forces in Albay
Location: Barangay Ibaugan, Daraga, Albay
Description: An NPA member handed over their weapons and explosives upon surrendering to government forces at Barangay Ibaugan, Daraga, Albay on 17 February 2026. The surrender was facilitated with the assistance of the 31st Infantry Battalion and the Philippine National Police under the Enhanced Comprehensive Local Integration Program (E-CLIP). Images showed the recovered items consisted of one Intratec TEC-9 submachine gun, two M76 rifle grenades, and approximately 250 rounds of ammunition. The 31st Infantry Battalion took custody of the weapons.
Weapons:
- 1 Intratec TEC-9 submachine gun
- 2 M76 rifle grenades
- approximately 250 rounds of ammunition
Actors:
NSAG (CPP-NPA)
Military (31st Infantry Battalion)
Police (PNP)

Title: NPA arms cache recovered by army and police in Buenavista, Quezon
Location: Between Brgys San Isidro Ilaya and Rizal, Buenavista, Quezon Province
Barangay: Between Brgys San Isidro Ilaya and Rizal
Description: Troops from the 85th Infantry (Sandiwa) Battalion, under the 201st Infantry (Kabalikat) Brigade, operating in coordination with the Philippine National Police in Quezon, recovered a buried arms cache at the boundary of Barangay San Isidro Ilaya and Barangay Rizal in Buenavista on 21 February. The operation was initiated following information provided by a former NPA member regarding the location of the concealed weapons. According to military reports, the firearms were buried following a series of armed encounters in Barangay Pansoy, San Andres, Quezon, and Barangay Huyon-Huyon, San Francisco, Quezon in January 2023. During their withdrawal, retreating NPA members buried two M16 rifles with attached M203 grenade launchers after exhausting their ammunition, and abandoned a third M16 rifle to hasten their movement. Intensified military operations and gunshot wounds sustained by some members of the group compelled them to leave the weapons behind. Security and clearing operations in the area are ongoing.
Weapons:
- 3 M16A1 assault rifles
- 2 M203 40mm grenade launchers
Actors:
NSAG (CPP-NPA)
Military (85th Infantry Battalion / 201st Infantry Brigade / 2nd Infantry Division, Philippine Army) 
Police (PNP Quezon)

Location: Barangay Tagbak, Talakag, Bukidnon
Description: An NPA member surrendered one craft shotgun and three rounds of ammunition to police in Barangay Tagbak, Talakag, Bukidnon on 21 February 2026. Location: San Mariano, Isabela

The surrender was attributed to sustained engagement efforts by the battalion and the government's continuing call for armed group members to abandon violence. Title: Police arrest alleged arms trafficker in buy-bust operation in Cebu City
Location: Abasolo White Road, Barangay Inayawan, Cebu City
Description: Personnel of the CIDG Lapu-Lapu City Field Unit and CIDG RFU-7 conducted a buy-bust operation on Abasolo White Road in Barangay Inayawan, Cebu City on 21 February 2026, resulting in the arrest of a 47-year-old male resident of Barangay Pardo. The suspect was allegedly selling unregistered firearms and was found in possession of one "Ingram M11"-style submachine gun, two caliber .45 pistols, and associated magazines. Two sachets of suspected shabu were also seized during the operation. The suspect is alleged to have been involved in gunrunning and drug activities in and around Cebu City.
Weapons:

- 2 caliber .45 pistols
Actors:
Criminal individual (arms trafficker, drug user)
Police (CIDG Lapu-Lapu City Field Unit, CIDG RFU-7)

Title: 83rd Infantry Battalion clashes with NPA in Presentacion, Camarines Sur
Location: Barangay Lidong, Presentacion, Camarines Sur
Description: Troops of the 83rd Infantry Battalion, operating under the 901st Infantry Brigade, conducted an operation against NPA elements in Barangay Lidong, Presentacion, Camarines Sur on 22 February 2026. The operation resulted in an armed encounter, after which troops seized one M16A1 rifle, one M14 rifle, dozens of rounds of ammunition, and an improvised explosive device. Traces of blood were discovered at the encounter site, indicating that NPA members sustained injuries during the clash.
Weapons:
- 1 M16A1 assault rifle
- 1 M14 rifle
- dozens of rounds of ammunition
- 1 improvised explosive device
Actors:
NSAG (CPP-NPA)
Military (83rd Infantry Battalion, 901st Infantry Brigade)

Title: Hit-and-run driver arrested with unlicensed pistol and shabu in Cavite
Location: Governors Drive, Barangay Maderan, General Mariano Alvarez, Cavite
Description: Police from the Carmona Component City Police Station and Cavite PPO seized an unlicensed 9mm Bersa Thunder Ultra Compact pistol and 22 rounds of ammunition, along with 75 grams of suspected shabu (estimated value ₱510,000) and drug paraphernalia, from a hit-and-run driver on Governors Drive in Barangay Maderan, General Mariano Alvarez, Cavite on 22 February 2026. The suspect collided with a motorcycle and fled the scene; police intercepted him in Barangay Maderan. Upon apprehending the suspect and retrieving his license, officers noticed a handgun inside his bag.
Weapons:
- 1 Bersa Thunder Ultra Compact 9mm pistol
- 22 rounds of ammunition
Actors:
Criminal individual (drug user)
Police (Carmona Component City Police Station, Cavite PPO)

Title: Communities in Zamboanga del Sur surrender 40 firearms and explosives to government
Location: Tukuran, Zamboanga del Sur
Description: Residents of Tukuran, Margosatubig, Tabina, Dinas, Dimataling, Pitogo, and Vincenzo A. Sagun in Zamboanga del Sur voluntarily surrendered 40 assorted firearms and explosives to the 53rd Infantry Battalion in Tukuran on 23 February 2026. The surrender was the eighth ceremony conducted in the province under the Small Arms and Light Weapons (SALW) Management Program, and children of participants were made eligible for provincial scholarship programs as an incentive. The surrender was attended by the Tukuran municipal government, the Office of the Presidential Adviser on Peace, Reconciliation and Unity, police, and military officials. Images corroborated a detailed manifest including one craft RPG-2, one M79 grenade launcher, two M14 rifles, two M1 carbines, seven M1 Garand rifles, four craft submachine guns, one SAS-12 shotgun, two craft shotguns, 15 pistols, five revolvers, eight hand grenades of mixed types (Mk2, M26, M67), two M76 rifle grenades, and four projected 40mm grenades.

- 1 M79 grenade launcher (serial defaced)
- 2 M14 rifles
- 2 M1 carbines
- 7 M1 Garand rifles

- 5 revolvers

Title: Joint AFP-PNP operation recovers M16 rifle from NPA arms cache in Balatan, Camarines Sur
Location: Barangay Cabanbanan, Balatan, Camarines Sur
Description: Troops of the 9th Infantry Battalion, 9th Infantry Division, supported by the PNP's 9th Special Action Battalion and Special Action Force, seized an M16A1 assault rifle, two pistols, a revolver, and 17 rounds of ammunition from a suspected NPA arms cache during an operation in Barangay Cabanbanan, Balatan, Camarines Sur on 23 February 2026. A former NPA member provided information about the cache location. The 9th Infantry Battalion took custody of all recovered items.

Title: 16 barangays surrender approximately 20 firearms to authorities in Datu Odin Sinsuat, Maguindanao del Norte
Location: Municipal Covered Court, Poblacion Dalican, Datu Odin Sinsuat, Maguindanao del Norte
Description: Residents of 16 barangays in Datu Odin Sinsuat, Maguindanao del Norte handed over approximately 20 weapons to authorities at the municipal covered court on 24 February 2026. The participating barangays were Baka (Datu Pinguiaman), Capiton, Tamontaka, Taviran, Badak, Linek, Mompong, Sapalan, Nekitan, Labungan, Tinunggos, Tambak, Dulangan, Kusiong, Kakar, and Binolen. The event was the second round of a municipal government surrender initiative that year; an earlier round in January had involved more than 20 assorted rifles and grenade launchers. The activity was led by Mayor Baba Abas (Abdulmain Abas) and Vice Mayor Datu Bobsteel Sinsuat, who reportedly reconciled ten feuding Moro clans that had engaged in deadly firefights due to land disputes, political rivalries, and family pride in recent years. Images showed one 9mm AR-pattern rifle, one M14 rifle, two M79 grenade launchers, one M79-style craft grenade launcher, one craft RPG-2-style launcher, three craft shotguns, one craft rifle, three craft submachine guns, two pistols, two revolvers, and one craft PG-2-style munition.
Weapons:
- 1 9mm AR-pattern rifle
- 1 M14 rifle
- 2 M79 grenade launchers
- 1 M79-style craft grenade launcher
- 1 craft RPG-2-style launcher
- 3 craft shotguns
- 1 craft rifle
- 3 craft submachine guns
- 2 pistols
- 2 revolvers
- 1 craft PG-2-style munition
Actors:
Civilian officials (Datu Odin Sinsuat municipal government)
Police (Maguindanao del Norte PPO, DOS Municipal Police Station)
Civilian community

Title: Buy-bust operation nets two drug suspects and firearm in Midland Village, Davao City
Location: Purok 42, Midland Village, Davao City
Description: Personnel from the Davao City Police Office's City Drug Enforcement Unit/Special Operations Group (CDEU/SOG-DCPO), in coordination with Regional Police Drug Enforcement Unit 11, the 1st and 2nd City Mobile Force Companies, the Ma-a Police Station, and the Philippine Drug Enforcement Agency Region XI, conducted a buy-bust operation in Purok 42, Midland Village, Davao City at approximately 1:48 a.m. on 25 February 2026. Two suspected drug dealers were arrested and approximately ₱68,000 worth of suspected shabu was seized. A caliber .45 firearm with three rounds of ammunition was also recovered during the operation.
Weapons:
- 1 caliber .45 pistol
- 3 rounds of ammunition
Actors:
Criminal individuals (2 suspected drug dealers)
Police (DCPO-CDEU/SOG, RPDEU XI, 1st and 2nd CMFC, Ma-a Police Station, PDEA Region XI)

Title: Assault rifle and pistol seized from sitting barangay kagawad in Lambayong, Sultan Kudarat
Location: Purok Liwa-Liwa, Barangay Palumbi, Lambayong, Sultan Kudarat
Description: Police from the CIDG Sultan Kudarat PFU 12 seized one select-fire Bushmaster M16 assault rifle, one caliber .45 pistol, and 17 rounds of ammunition when they searched the residence of Frederich Joey Gacula Pande, a sitting barangay kagawad of Barangay Palumbi, Lambayong, Sultan Kudarat on 25 February 2026. The search was conducted as part of a campaign against suspected private armed groups in Sultan Kudarat. Police also arrested Pande's house guard, Antonio Orlanda Biala. Neither suspect could present valid licensing documents for the firearms.

Title: Farmer arrested in Surallah, South Cotabato for illegal drugs and firearms
Location: Purok Asparagus, Barangay Colongulo, Surallah, South Cotabato
Description: Joint elements of the Surallah Municipal Police Station, the South Cotabato Provincial Intelligence Team, Regional Intelligence Unit 12, and the Philippine Drug Enforcement Agency Region XII served a court-issued search warrant on 25 February 2026 at the residence of Winston Calawigan (alias "Weng-Weng"), a 43-year-old married farmer in Purok Asparagus, Barangay Colongulo, Surallah, South Cotabato. The search recovered approximately 1.80 grams of suspected shabu and 2.73 grams of dried marijuana leaves (combined estimated value over ₱14,000), drug paraphernalia, a Taurus caliber 9mm pistol with a steel magazine, a caliber .45 pistol, and 88 rounds of assorted ammunition. Images showed one pistol and ammunition, indicating only one firearm was visible in documented evidence.
Weapons:
- 1 Taurus 9mm pistol (with steel magazine)
- [report also describes 1 caliber .45 pistol; only 1 pistol visible in images]
- 88 rounds of assorted ammunition
Actors:
Criminal individual (drug user)

Title: Regional-level drug suspect arrested in buy-bust operation in Ecoland, Davao City
Location: Parakeet Street, Barangay 76-A, Ecoland Phase 2, Davao City
Description: Operatives of the Davao City Police Office's City Drug Enforcement Unit conducted a buy-bust operation on Parakeet Street in Barangay 76-A, Ecoland Phase 2, Davao City at approximately 4:22 p.m. on 25 February 2026. The operation resulted in the arrest of a 45-year-old male, identified by the aliases "Melvin" and "Bin," a resident of Purok 6, Lanao, Kidapawan City, who had been classified as a Regional Level Top Priority Illegal Drug Personality. Two sachets of suspected shabu with an estimated value of ₱102,000 were seized, along with a Smith & Wesson caliber .38 revolver loaded with three live rounds, a PNP License to Own and Possess Firearms (LTOPF) ID, and ₱1,000 in cash.
Weapons:
- 1 Smith & Wesson caliber .38 revolver
- 3 rounds of ammunition
Actors:
Criminal organization (drug trafficker)
Police (DCPO-CDEU)

Title: Police arrest two foreign nationals in gun buy-bust in Pasay City
Location: J.W. Diokno Boulevard, Pasay City, Metro Manila
Description: Personnel of the CIDG Southern Metro Manila District Field Unit arrested a Chinese national and a Taiwanese national during a buy-bust operation at a restaurant on J.W. Diokno Boulevard in Pasay City, Metro Manila on 25 February 2026. One 9mm revolver and three rounds of ammunition were seized. The operation followed intelligence that a foreign national was selling unregistered firearms. PNP intelligence units were investigating whether the two suspects are connected to larger firearms syndicates operating in and around Metro Manila.
Weapons:
- 1 9mm revolver
- 3 rounds of ammunition

Criminal organization (Chinese/Taiwanese nationals, arms traffickers)
Police (CIDG Southern Metro Manila District Field Unit)

Title: Man arrested for illegal possession of firearm in Barangay Bayanan, Muntinlupa City
Location: San Guillermo Street, Barangay Bayanan, Muntinlupa City
Description: Operatives of the Muntinlupa Police Office's Alabang Sub-station arrested a 33-year-old male, identified by the alias "Kim" and a resident of Barangay Bayanan, on San Guillermo Street in the same barangay at approximately 4:50 p.m. on 25 February 2026. The arrest followed a report that the individual was armed and causing fear among residents. A caliber .38 pistol and two rounds of live ammunition were confiscated. The suspect faces charges under Republic Act No. 10591.

Title: Police and troops seize HK416 and M14 rifles from suspected traffickers in Zamboanga City
Location: Zamora Intersection, Barangay Pasonanca, Zamboanga City
Description: Police and military personnel seized one HK416 assault rifle and two M14 rifles when they arrested two men transporting the weapons at the Zamora Intersection in Barangay Pasonanca, Zamboanga City on 26 February 2026. The suspects, from Cotabato City and driving a rented vehicle, initially attempted to flee a checkpoint in Barangay Santa Maria before being pursued and intercepted. Police and military officials believe the weapons originated from Datu Odin Sinsuat, Maguindanao del Norte, and were being delivered to Zamboanga City.
Weapons:
- 1 HK416 assault rifle
- 2 M14 rifles

Criminal individuals (arms traffickers)

Military (1st Marine Rifle Battalion; 6th Infantry Division)

Location: Barangay Kamalig, Panamao, Sulu
Description: Troops of the 21st Infantry Battalion and personnel from the Panamao Municipal Police Station responded to a report of a shooting incident in Barangay Kamalig, Panamao, Sulu on 26 February 2026. A caliber .45 pistol was seized following the operation. The suspect was arrested without further violence. Images corroborated the recovery of one M1911 pistol bearing US Army markings.

Title: Former rebel surrenders Vz58 rifles and craft RPG-2 launcher in Malaybalay City, Bukidnon
Location: Camp Captain Ramon M. Onahon, Malaybalay City, Bukidnon

The surrender took place under the Enhanced Comprehensive Local Integration Program (E-CLIP) and was overseen by the Bukidnon PPO, the 8th Infantry Battalion, 4th Infantry Division, and the DILG. Title: Retired police officer dies from grenade blast in domestic disturbance in Island Garden City of Samal
Location: Barangay San Remigio, Kaputian District, Island Garden City of Samal, Davao del Norte
Description: A retired police officer was shot by responding officers and died from a hand grenade blast during an altercation in Barangay San Remigio, Kaputian District, Island Garden City of Samal on 26 February 2026. The incident began when the suspect arrived armed at a neighbour's residence, where an argument escalated into the suspect shooting a female victim in the leg. Responding officers from the Island Garden City of Samal Police Station attempted to negotiate, but the suspect continued firing and armed a hand grenade with its safety pin removed. Officers engaged the suspect, who was shot and subsequently dropped the grenade, which detonated near his body. He was declared dead on arrival at hospital. The female victim was evacuated. Police recovered a caliber .45 pistol and remnants of the hand grenade.
Weapons:
- 1 caliber .45 pistol
- 1 hand grenade (detonated)
Actors:
Former police/soldier (retired police officer)
Police (Island Garden City of Samal Police Station)

Title: Four armed men arrested with M16 rifle, M14 rifles, and grenades in Talipao, Sulu
Location: Barangay Bagsak, Talipao, Sulu
Description: Troops of the 41st Infantry Battalion and police from PNP Talipao arrested four individuals and seized an array of weapons in Barangay Bagsak, Talipao, Sulu on 27 February 2026, following a complaint from residents about armed men in the area. The four suspects — identified as Jesfar Ablahan Jarah, Abdel Naser Sumaidi, Ahamad Hamsarani, and Kiram Muntanyo Sahiron — were allegedly involved in a fatal shooting incident in Barangay Bilaan, Talipao in September 2025. The operation was conducted under Talipao's Gun-Free and Peace-Centered Community Ordinance, which had taken effect on 17 February 2026. Images confirmed the seizure of two M14 rifles, one M16 assault rifle with a defaced serial number, one M203 grenade launcher, five projected 40mm grenades, and 220 rounds of ammunition.
Weapons:
- 2 M14 rifles
- 1 M16 assault rifle (serial number defaced)

- 220 rounds of ammunition
Actors:

Title: Authorities seize grenade from BIFF field commander in Tupi, South Cotabato
Location: Purok 1, Barangay Bunao, Tupi, South Cotabato
Description: Troops and police seized a caliber .45 pistol and one Mk2 hand grenade when they arrested a field commander of the BIFF-Bungos Faction in Purok 1, Barangay Bunao, Tupi, South Cotabato on 27 February 2026. The suspect, Nasrullah Omar Onsek, had been a wanted individual since October 2021 on charges of multiple attempted murder, destructive arson, and violations of the Anti-Terrorism Act of 2020. Onsek is noted for his expertise in improvised explosive device fabrication. The operation was conducted by the Tupi Municipal Police Station and CIDG RFO 12, supported by the 6th Infantry Battalion, 1st Mechanized Infantry Brigade, 6th Infantry Division.
Weapons:
- 1 caliber .45 pistol
- 1 Mk2 hand grenade
Actors:

Title: Government Forces Seize Firearms From Feuding Moro Groups in North Cotabato, SGA-BARMM
Location: Barangay Lomopog, Midsayap, North Cotabato; Barangay Kudarangan, Nabalawag, SGA-BARMM
Barangay: Barangay Lomopog, Midsayap, North Cotabato; Barangay Kudarangan, Nabalawag, SGA-BARMM
Description:
Troops and police personnel seized over 20 firearms, including a K3 light machine gun and an 81mm mortar, during clearing operations in Barangay Lomopog, Midsayap, North Cotabato and the adjacent Nabalawag boundary area of the SGA-BARMM on 6 March 2026, following armed clashes between two rival Moro factions.

The underlying conflict involved an armed group associated with Nabalawag Mayor Renz Tukuran and another led by an individual named Commander Kuntay, reportedly affiliated with the MILF. The groups had been engaged in a recurring feud over control of agricultural land along the Midsayap–Nabalawag border, aggravated by support for rival candidates in the May 2025 elections. The factions clashed on March 3 and 4, killing a 19-year-old female vendor in Barangay Damatulan, wounding five members of a family in Barangay Duaminanga, and displacing over 2,000 residents. Local officials and community leaders said that both factions lost two members each (four total), and that two civilians were killed in the crossfire, according to PhilStar Global.

On March 5, clashes took place near Barangay Kadigasan, Nabalawag and Barangay Lomopog, Midsayap. Government forces launched an operation on March 6 to arrest individuals linked to the clashes. While conducting the operation, armed men inside a residential compound opened fire on government forces, triggering a brief firefight. The 13 firearms were subsequently recovered during clearing operations; nine of these weapons were found at the residence of Mayor Tukuran during a search overseen by traditional Moro leaders. Rifle grenade rounds, hand fragmentation grenades, bandoliers, magazines, and various ammunition and explosives were also recovered. The operation was conducted in close coordination with the MILF's Coordinating Committee on the Cessation of Hostilities (CCCH) and the Ad Hoc Joint Action Group (AHJAG). On March 7, military and MILF officials brokered a further ceasefire between the two factions.

On 8 March, the authorities displayed the seized weapons at a press conference at Camp Siongco, showing 33 firearms recovered across operations on March 5–6 in Maguindanao del Sur, Cotabato, and SGA-BARMM — specifically at Barangay Linantangan, Shariff Saydona Mustapha; Barangays Manongkaling and Sapakan, Mamasapano, Maguindanao del Sur; Barangay Lomopog, Midsayap, Cotabato; and Barangay Kudarangan, Nabalawag, SGA-BARMM. One source breaks this down as 23 firearms attributed to the 33rd Infantry Battalion (presumably in the Lomopog-Nabalawag axis) and 10 to the 34th Infantry Battalion (presumably in the Sapakan axis; see PHL.26.03.06.002).

According to photos from the press event, the weapons seized in Lomopog included one 81mm M29 mortar, one K3 light machine gun, two BAR light machine guns, one M4 assault rifle, two M16A1 assault rifles, five M14 rifles, one M1 Garand rifle, around 900 rounds of belted ammunition (including .50 caliber rounds), one M26 hand grenade, two PRB-423 hand grenades, at least eight 40mm projected grenades, and five M76 rifle grenades. The weapons seized from Kudarangan included one M60 light machine gun (s/n 7229229; MNLF flag visible on the buttstock), two M4 assault rifles, five M16A1 assault rifles, two M14 rifles, and one M1 Garand.
Weapons:
- 1 81mm M29 mortar
- 1 K3 light machine gun
- 2 BAR light machine guns
- 1 M60 light machine gun
- 3 M4 assault rifles
- 7 M16A1 assault rifles
- 7 M14 rifles
- 2 M1 Garand rifles
- 1 M26 hand grenade
- 2 PRB-423 hand grenades
- 5 M76 rifle grenades
- 8 projected 40mm grenades
- ~900 rounds of ammunition
Actors:
Communal/Political Militia (Mayor Renz Tukuran-affiliated group; Commander Kuntay-affiliated group)
Military (602nd Infantry Brigade, 33rd Infantry Battalion, 6th Infantry Division, Joint Task Force Central)
Police (Midsayap Municipal Police Station)
NSAG (MILF-CCCH, AHJAG)

Title: Troops Seize M16 Rifles After Clashes Between MILF-linked Groups in Maguindanao del Sur
Location: Barangay Sapakan, Mamasapano, Maguindanao del Sur
Description:
Troops of the 34th Infantry Battalion seized four M16 assault rifles and ammunition following clashes between MILF-BIAF-affiliated groups in Barangay Sapakan, Mamasapano, Maguindanao del Sur on 6 March 2026. The weapons were thought to have been abandoned during the clashes.

Elements of the 34th Infantry Battalion, together with police units from Mamasapano, Rajah Buayan, and Shariff Aguak, launched a Joint Law Enforcement Support Operation (LESO) after two armed groups with ties to the Bangsamoro Islamic Armed Forces (BIAF) of the Moro Islamic Liberation Front clashed. Following de-escalation and clearing operations, four M16 rifles along with magazines, bandoliers, and ammunition were recovered, believed to have been abandoned following the armed confrontation. The Coordinating Committee on the Cessation of Hostilities (CCCH) monitored the operation. No civilians were reported injured during the clearing.

On 8 March, the authorities displayed the seized weapons at a press conference at Camp Siongco, showing 33 firearms recovered across operations on March 5–6 in Maguindanao del Sur, Cotabato, and SGA-BARMM — specifically at Barangay Linantangan, Shariff Saydona Mustapha; Barangays Manongkaling and Sapakan, Mamasapano, Maguindanao del Sur; Barangay Lomopog, Midsayap, Cotabato; and Barangay Kudarangan, Nabalawag, SGA-BARMM. One source breaks this down as 23 firearms attributed to the 33rd Infantry Battalion (presumably in the Lomopog-Nabalawag axis; see PHL.26.03.06.001) and 10 to the 34th Infantry Battalion (presumably in the Sapakan axis).

According to photos from the press event, the weapons seized in Sapakan included four M16A1 assault rifles. The weapons seized in Manongkaling and Linantangan included two M16A1 assault rifles and four M14 rifles.
Weapons:
- 6 M16A1 assault rifles
- 4 M14 rifles
- at least 40 rounds of ammunition
Actors:
Communal/Political Militia (MILF-BIAF-affiliated armed groups)
Military (34th Infantry Battalion, 601st Infantry Brigade, 6th Infantry Division)

Description: Authorities arrested three suspected arms traffickers and seized two high-powered firearms during a buy-bust operation in Barangay Bayanga Norte, Matanog, Maguindanao del Norte on 8 March 2026. The operation was conducted as part of intensified efforts to curb the proliferation of loose firearms in the province.
Weapons:
- 2 AR-pattern assault rifles (described as Colt and Singaporean CIS)
- 13 rounds of ammunition
Actors:
Criminal Individual(s) (arms traffickers)
Police (Matanog Municipal Police Station, Maguindanao del Norte PPO)

Title: Authorities Seize Assault Rifles, Grenade Launcher from Abandoned Vehicle in Lanao del Sur
Location: Barangay Curahab, Malabang, Lanao del Sur
Description:
Troops and police recovered six firearms and large quantities of ammunition from an abandoned minivan (plate no. KBF 8095) during clearing operations in Barangay Curahab, Malabang, Lanao del Sur on 8 March 2026, after responding to reports of indiscriminate firing in the area. The vehicle had no registration documents and its owner could not be determined; follow-up investigations were underway to identify those responsible. The items were to be submitted to the PNP Crime Laboratory in Marawi City for ballistic examination.

Note one M16A1 assault rifle had an 'RP' serial number, indicating it originated from a government stockpile.
Weapons:
- 1 Smith & Wesson M&P assault rifle (S/N 01223)
- 1 M16A1 assault rifle (S/N RP133775)
- 1 M16A1 assault rifle (S/N 467309)
- 1 AR-pattern assault rifle (S/N 145298437)
- 1 Colt 653 assault rifle (S/N 283488)
- 1 M203 grenade launcher (S/N 133627)
- 1 M14 rifle (S/N 1212414)
- 18 40mm projected grenades
- 522 rounds of ammunition
Actors:
Unknown/Unaffiliated Entity (possibly communal/political militia)
Military (51st Infantry Battalion, 101st Infantry Brigade, 1st Infantry Division)
Police (Malabang Municipal Police Station, 2nd PMFC, Lanao del Sur PPO)

Title: Ex-Police Officer Kills Police Intelligence Officer in Suspected Hit in Bulacan
Location: Brooklyn Heights Subdivision, Barangay Panginay, Guiguinto, Bulacan
Description:
A dismissed police officer (Marvin Salim, 43) fatally shot a police intelligence officer, Master Sgt. Jose Uring (50), and wounded a civilian (Jonilyn Acedera, 54) at a homeowners association caucus in Brooklyn Heights Subdivision, Barangay Panginay, Guiguinto, Bulacan on 8 March 2026. Salim was identified as a former officer previously assigned at the Obando Police Station; his alleged driver, Adonis Santos (42), was arrested along with him during hot pursuit operations in Bulakan and Obando on 10 March 2026, less than 48 hours after the shooting.

Investigators seized three pistols (a Remington 1911 caliber .45, a Caspian caliber .45, and a Smith & Wesson 9mm), ammunition, a grenade, motorcycles, and other evidence. Authorities are investigating whether the suspects were hired gunmen with a mastermind behind the attack. The motive is believed to be connected to rivalry over the homeowners association election, in which Uring was seeking a third term as president.
Weapons:
- 1 Remington 1911 caliber .45 pistol
- 1 Caspian caliber .45 pistol
- 1 Smith & Wesson 9mm pistol
- 1 grenade (type not specified in sources)
- assorted ammunition
Actors:
Former Police/Soldier (Marvin Salim, dismissed police officer)
Criminal Individual(s) (hitmen)
Police (Guiguinto MPS, Bulacan PPO, Provincial Intelligence Unit, Bulakan and Obando Police Stations, 1st PMFC, RSOG3, RIU3)

Title: Barangay Officials Arrested for Illegal Firearms Sale in Batangas
Location: Barangay Janao-Janao, San Juan, Batangas
Description: Police arrested a sitting barangay kagawad and two barangay tanods and seized four caliber .45 pistols during a buy-bust operation in Barangay Janao-Janao, San Juan, Batangas on 9 March 2026. Three pistols were seized at the time of the operation; a fourth was recovered from one of the suspects during a body search. Authorities allege the suspects were involved in gunrunning activities operating in the province.
Weapons:
- 4 caliber .45 pistols
- 44 rounds of ammunition
Actors:
Political Affiliate (barangay kagawad, two barangay tanods)
Police (CIDG Batangas PFU, San Juan Municipal Police Station)

Barangay: Barangay Luuk Tara (and Barangay Duhol Tara)
Description: Residents of Luuk Tara and Duhol Tara voluntarily surrendered one M1 Garand rifle, one M1 Carbine rifle, and one M79 grenade launcher to troops of the 104th Infantry Battalion and the Siasi Municipal Police Station on 10 March 2026, as part of the municipality's Rido-Free, Gun-Free, and Peace-Centered Community Campaign.

Title: Police Seize Firearms and Grenade From NPA Members in Tarlac City
Location: Barangay Baras-Baras, Tarlac City
Description: Police arrested seven CPP-NPA members and seized three unlicensed firearms and one hand grenade during an operation in Barangay Baras-Baras, Tarlac City on 11 March 2026. Five of the suspects were arrested on active warrants for Attempted Murder and Qualified Trafficking in Persons issued by courts in Kalinga, Ilocos Sur, and Abra between 2018 and 2020; two others were apprehended in flagrante delicto for illegal possession of firearms and explosives. Authorities also confiscated mobile phones, SIM cards, a USB flash drive, and cash.
Weapons:
- 1 M16A1 assault rifle
- 1 caliber .45 pistol
- 1 caliber .38 revolver
- 1 Mk2 hand grenade
- ammunition

Title: Troops Seize R4 Assault Rifle From NPA Arms Cache in Surigao del Sur
Location: Sitio Lago, Barangay Carromata, San Miguel, Surigao del Sur
Description: Troops of the 30th Infantry Battalion seized one R4 rifle and ammunition when they unearthed an NPA arms cache at Sitio Lago, Barangay Carromata, San Miguel, Surigao del Sur on 11 March 2026. The weapons were attributed to the dismantled Sub-Regional Sentro de Grabidad (SRSDG) Westland unit of the Northeastern Mindanao Regional Committee. The discovery led to a second arms cache found at a nearby sitio on 13 March (see PHL.2026.03.13.001).

Description: Troops of the 55th Infantry Battalion and the 64th Infantry Battalion seized 295 hand grenades from a DI-MG arms cache in Barangay Piagolongan, Marogong, Lanao del Sur on 11 March 2026. The cache was located with the assistance of information provided by local residents. The recovered grenades were deposited at the 55th IB headquarters for safekeeping and subsequent ordnance disposal.

Title: Police Recover M16A1 from Shooting Suspects' Residence in Lanao del Sur
Location: Barangay Tubok, Malabang, Lanao del Sur
Description: Government forces seized one Elisco M16A1 assault rifle and three rounds of ammunition from a residence in Barangay Tubok, Malabang, Lanao del Sur on 11 March 2026. The seizure took place following a shooting earlier that day in Barangay Curahab in which Safra Pangsayan Bantuas was wounded. The identified suspects — Alinor Malatus Bato and an alias "Itlog" — fled the area before the authorities arrived. The firearm was found in plain view inside the house believed to be linked to the suspects, and was turned over to the Provincial Forensic Unit for ballistic examination.
Weapons:
- 1 Elisco M16A1 assault rifle
- 3 rounds of ammunition
Actors:
Criminal Individual(s) (shooting suspects)
Police (Malabang MPS, 2nd PMFC, Lanao del Sur PPO)

Title: Three NPA Members Surrender with Firearms in Bukidnon
Location: Barangay Halapitan, San Fernando, Bukidnon
Description: Three CPP-NPA members surrendered a homemade 12-gauge shotgun and two caliber .45 pistols to police at Barangay Halapitan, San Fernando, Bukidnon on 11 March 2026. The former militants — identified by aliases "Bogtong," "Mike" and "Rolyn" — were members of the dismantled Squad Uno, Platoon 3, Guerrilla Front Malayag, Sub-Regional Committee 2, North Central Mindanao Regional Committee. They are being processed for enrollment in the E-CLIP reintegration program.
Weapons:

Title: Police Seize Eight Firearms From Security Guard in Quezon

Weapons:

- 2 caliber 9mm pistols
- 3 12-gauge shotguns

Title: NPA Claims Two Firearms Captured in Negros Occidental
Location: Between Kabankalan, Sipalay, Cauayan, and Candoni, Southwest Negros Occidental
Description: The CPP-NPA claimed fighters of the Armando Sumayang Jr. Command captured two unspecified firearms from individuals described as "counter-revolutionaries and bad elements" during separate operations in Southwest Negros Occidental on 12 and 19 March 2026. The claim was disclosed in a communique published on 4 April 2026 reporting five NPA military actions during the month of March, and no independent corroboration is available. No specific locations, firearm types, or per-incident breakdown of the seized weapons were provided.
Weapons: - 2 unspecified firearms
Actors: NSAG (CPP-NPA, Armando Sumayang Jr. Command/Southwest Negros)

Barangay: Sitio Comarobcob (also referred to as Sitio Gis Anan), Barangay Carromata

Title: Two Suspects Arrested After Triple Murder of Maranao Gold Buyers in Cagayan
Location: Barangay San Juan, Buguey, Cagayan
Description:
Police arrested two robbery suspects — Frederick Usana (31) and Rodel Sabal (48), both of Buguey — in Barangay San Juan, Buguey, Cagayan on 13 March 2026. The suspects are believed to have robbed and murdered three Maranao gold buyers (Salic, Mutalib, and Janisah Amerol) in Barangay Sta. Teresa, Lal-lo, Cagayan the day before. The victims had traveled from Tuguegarao City for a purported gold sale transaction worth ₱1.2 million; authorities allege the meeting was a premeditated robbery. Approximately ₱1.1 million in cash was reported missing.

Recovered from the suspects were a 9mm pistol, one M16 assault rifle with a defaced serial number, improvised shotguns, one rifle grenade, and ammunition. One suspect (Usana) tested positive in a paraffin test. A third suspect, Michel John Rubiaco, remained at large. The suspects reportedly had previous business dealings with the victims.
Weapons:
- 1 9mm Taurus G3 pistol
- 1 M16A1 assault rifle
- 2 craft shotguns
- 1 rifle grenade (presumed M76)
- 33 rounds of ammunition
Actors:
Criminal Individual(s) (robbery-murder perpetrators)
Police (Buguey MPS, Cagayan PPO, Police Regional Office 2)

Title: Pistol Seized From Man for Illegal Firearm Possession in Agusan del Sur
Location: Purok 4, Barangay Taglatawan, Bayugan City, Agusan del Sur
Description: Police arrested a 38-year-old man (alias "Jeffer") from Prosperidad, Agusan del Sur and seized one caliber .45 pistol and eight rounds of ammunition in Barangay Taglatawan, Bayugan City on 13 March 2026. The suspect was intercepted while traveling on his way back to Prosperidad.
Weapons:
- 1 caliber .45 pistol
- 8 rounds of ammunition
Actors:
Criminal Individual(s) (illegal firearm possession)
Police (Bayugan City Police Station)

Title: Troops Seize Rifle, 40mm Grenades From NPA Arms Cache in Panay

Barangay: Hinterland boundaries of Libacao, Aklan; Iloilo; and Capiz
Description: Troops of the 82nd Infantry Battalion seized an NPA arms cache between Libacao, Aklan, Iloilo, and Capiz on 14 March 2026. The weapons belonged to the Komiteng Rehiyon-Panay (KR-P) faction of the CPP-NPA. The cache was located through information provided by local residents and with the assistance of former rebels. In addition to a firearm, the cache contained a substantial quantity of ammunition and specialized magazines.
Weapons:
- 1 M1 Garand rifle
- 157 rounds of ammunition
- 2 40mm projected grenades
Actors:
NSAG (CPP-NPA, Komiteng Rehiyon-Panay)
Military (82nd Infantry Battalion, 3rd Infantry Division)

Title: Three BIFF/DI Members Surrender Weapons to NBI in Maguindanao del Sur
Location: Mamasapano, Maguindanao del Sur
Description: The National Bureau of Investigation facilitated the surrender of three members of the "BIFF/DI Karialan Faction" in Mamasapano, Maguindanao del Sur on 14 March 2026. The individuals sought a discreet channel for surrender due to fear of retaliation from other militants. During the surrender, they turned over two rifles, two grenade launchers, and two rounds of ammunition. Following the extraction, they were transported to a secure facility for debriefing and security processing before enrollment in a reintegration program.
Weapons:
- 2 craft rifles
- 1 craft grenade launcher
- 1 M203 grenade launcher
- 2 rounds of ammunition
Actors:
NSAG (BIFF/DI, Karialan faction)
Police (NBI, NBI-BARMM, NBI-CEMRO, NBI-SARDO, NBI Counter Terrorism Division)

Title: Construction Worker Discovers WWII-Era Aerial Bomb in Davao City
Location: Sitio Sunflower, Barangay Mandug Proper, Buhangin District, Davao City
Barangay: Sitio Sunflower, Barangay Mandug Proper, Buhangin District
Description: A construction worker unearthed a vintage munition while digging a fish trap pit in Sitio Sunflower, Barangay Mandug Proper, Buhangin, Davao City on 14 March 2026. Initially believing the metallic object might be a container holding valuables, he and a companion began digging it out before recognizing its appearance as a bomb and calling 911. The Davao City Explosive and Canine Unit (CECU) responded, identified the device as a JP-250 (a 250-kilogram class vintage aerial bomb, likely a World War II relic), and safely transported it for proper disposal.
Weapons: - 1 JP-250 vintage 250-kilogram aerial bomb (WWII-era munition)
Actors:
Civilian Community (Mandug Proper)
Police (Davao City CECU, Buhangin Police Station, CTOC)

Title: Man Arrested for Illegal Firearm Possession at Checkpoint in Maguindanao del Sur
Location: Barangay Baital, Rajah Buayan, Maguindanao del Sur
Description: Police arrested a man and seized one Armscor caliber .45 pistol (S/N 25371) and four rounds of ammunition at a checkpoint in Barangay Baital, Rajah Buayan, Maguindanao del Sur on 14 March 2026. The suspect failed to present any legal authority to possess or carry the firearm and is facing charges for violation of the Comprehensive Firearms and Ammunition Regulation Act. The weapon is to be submitted to the Regional Forensic Unit for ballistic examination.
Weapons:
- 1 Armscor caliber .45 pistol (S/N 25371)
- 4 rounds of ammunition
Actors:
Criminal Individual(s) (illegal firearm possession)
Police (Rajah Buayan Municipal Police Station)

Title: Taxi Driver Surrenders Abandoned Revolver Found Along Highway in Cabadbaran City
Location: Poblacion 5, Cabadbaran City, Agusan del Norte
Description: A taxi driver from Butuan City surrendered a Smith & Wesson .38 Special revolver he found along the National Highway in Cabadbaran City to personnel of the PNP-HPG Provincial Highway Patrol Team, Agusan del Norte on 14 March 2026. The driver discovered an unattended sling bag containing the firearm and ammunition while stopped briefly along the roadside after dropping off a passenger, and turned it over out of concern about its potential misuse. The firearm — with a defaced serial number — is under the custody of the PHPT and will undergo verification by the Regional Crime Scene Unit 13.
Weapons:
- 1 Smith & Wesson .38 Special revolver
- 2 rounds of .38 caliber live ammunition
Actors:
Civilian Community (taxi driver, finder and voluntary surrenderer)
Police (PNP-HPG, Provincial Highway Patrol Team Agusan del Norte)

Title: Two NPA Members Surrender with M1 Carbine Rifles in South Cotabato
Location: Barangay Bula, General Santos City, South Cotabato
Description: Two CPP-NPA members of Team B, Platoon Brigol of the dismantled Guerrilla Front (GF) 73/GF Musa surrendered two M1 Carbine rifles with magazines to troops in Barangay Bula, General Santos City on 14 March 2026. The militants stated that they sought to clear their names and sever all ties with the NPA. They were formally presented to Brigadier General Michael A. Santos, Commander of the 603rd Infantry Brigade, and General Santos City Mayor Lorelie G. Pacquiao at a ceremony held on 16 March 2026 at the City Agriculturist Office, after which they received financial assistance and food packs from DSWD Region XII. They are being enrolled in the E-CLIP program.
Weapons: - 2 M1 Carbine rifles
Actors:
NSAG (CPP-NPA, Guerrilla Front 73/GF Musa)
Military (603rd Infantry Brigade, Task Force Gensan, Philippine Army)

Title: Troops Seize Assault Rifles from NPA Arms Cache in Bukidnon
Location: Sitio Ananaso, Barangay Kalabugao, Impasug-ong, Bukidnon
Description: Troops of the 1st Special Forces Battalion unearthed two fully serviceable firearms abandoned by CPP-NPA elements at Sitio Ananaso, Barangay Kalabugao, Impasug-ong, Bukidnon on 15 March 2026, acting on intelligence provided by a former rebel supporter. Recovered were a 5.56mm Elisco M16A1 rifle with a defaced serial number and a 5.56mm Elisco M653 carbine bearing serial number RP254466 (indicating origin from a state stockpile). Both firearms were transported to the 1st Special Forces Battalion headquarters for documentation and ballistic tracing. Military officials attributed the cache to NPA members who abandoned the weapons while evading sustained security operations in the area.
Weapons:

- 1 Elisco M653 assault rifle (S/N RP254466)
Actors:
NSAG (CPP-NPA)
Military (1st Special Forces Battalion, 403rd Infantry Brigade, 4th Infantry Division)

Location: Barangay Bas Nonok, Lugus, Sulu

Title: Nine Residents Surrender Firearms to Authorities in Poona Bayabao, Lanao del Sur
Location: Barangay Bansayan, Poona Bayabao, Lanao del Sur

Location: Barangay Tubig Putih, Luuk, Sulu
Description: A civilian voluntarily surrendered one caliber .30 M1 Garand rifle to the 101st Infantry Battalion in Barangay Tubig Putih, Luuk, Sulu on 17 March 2026, as part of ongoing efforts to establish Luuk as a Gun-Free and Peace-Centered Municipality. The turnover was facilitated by Barangay Chairperson Hon. Rudzna W. Sabbarani in coordination with the Luuk Municipal Police Station.

Title: NPA Members Reveal Arms Cache with M60 Machine Gun to Troops in Camarines Norte

Description:
Three CPP-NPA members (aliases "Bloody," "Calo," and "Sam/Riza") surrendered to government forces in Barangays Mahawanhawan and Baay, Labo, Camarines Norte on 17 March 2026. The operation, conducted from 6:00 AM to 4:30 PM, involved 14 personnel from the 91st Special Action Company and the 9th Special Action Battalion (PNP-SAF) providing tactical support to the 42nd Infantry Battalion as the lead unit.

During custodial debriefing, the militants disclosed the location of an arms cache in Sitio Malapat, Barangay Baay. Government forces subsequently recovered one caliber 7.62mm M60 machine gun (defaced serial number), two caliber 5.56mm M16 assault rifles (defaced serial numbers), and 300 rounds of ammunition. The surrenderers held positions as vice squad leader and assistant platoon guide within their unit.

Both cited severe food shortages, dwindling community support, and sustained military pressure as reasons for their surrender. Hukman had been wounded in an encounter with government forces on 10 January 2026 in Barangay Cayale, Tago, Surigao del Sur, which forced him to separate from his unit. Location: Sitio Santol, Barangay Tampalon, Kabankalan City, Negros Occidental
Description:
Troops of the 47th Infantry Battalion killed three NPA members and recovered five firearms and one grenade in Sitio Santol, Barangay Tampalon, Kabankalan City, Negros Occidental on 21 March 2026. The firefight, which lasted approximately two minutes, involved approximately seven NPA militants; four fled. No government casualties were reported.

The three fatalities were identified as Ritchie Verano (alias "Makoy/Akiro/Venom," 26, Vice Commander of the Regional Striking Force), Milky Sampini (alias "Gorting," 27–29, Finance and Logistics Officer of RSF), and Joedil Balsimo (alias "Junjun/Cairo," Squad Leader of RSF). The group was linked to a series of killings and arson attacks in Negros Island, including the deaths of 74-year-old Leonora Anguit and 39-year-old Renante Vedarte, both of Barangay Tapi, Kabankalan City. The operation was triggered by community reports of armed NPA presence in the area.

- 6 40mm projected grenades

The operation was launched following reports from local residents about extortion and threats by the group. The surviving rebel elements fled after the firefight, reportedly carrying three wounded companions. Philippine Inquirer also reported two government soldiers were wounded.

The fatalities were identified as Ronnie Salahay (alias "Ka Bunso"), Squad 3 leader of SRSDG Westland, NEMRC, and Jerome Suazo Montenegro (alias "Ka Rex"), Squad 1 member of SRSDG Westland, NEMRC. Sources indicate that Salahay had reportedly wanted to surrender but was prevented by threats from his commanders against his family.

Title: Police Seize Grenade from Suspected Criminals in Sultan Kudarat, Maguindanao del Norte
Location: Barangay Rebuken, Sultan Kudarat, Maguindanao del Norte
Description:
Two wanted persons — Peter D. Osorio (35, of Antipas, North Cotabato) and Edwin J. Mallorca (56, of Barangay Rebuken) — were killed in a shootout with police operatives serving an arrest warrant in Barangay Rebuken, Sultan Kudarat, Maguindanao del Norte on 23 March 2026. According to police, barking dogs alerted the suspects of the police approach; Osorio subsequently opened fire while Mallorca attempted to throw a grenade, prompting officers to return fire. Both suspects were critically wounded and declared dead on arrival at hospital.

Recovered from the scene were one caliber .45 pistol, one hand grenade, and 12 sachets of suspected shabu (estimated value ₱7,700). Osorio had been facing a warrant for multiple murder issued by the Regional Trial Court and was ranked Top 8 in the PRO-12 most wanted list; both men were linked to more than ten killings-for-hire in Sultan Kudarat, Sultan Mastura, and Cotabato City since late 2024. Mallorca was also described as a drug trafficker.
Weapons:
- 1 caliber .45 pistol (S/N 118725)
- 1 Mk2 hand grenade
Actors:
Criminal Individual(s) (murder suspects, drug traffickers)
Police (Sultan Kudarat MPS, Police Regional Office-Bangsamoro Autonomous Region)

Title: Police Seize Two Revolvers at Murder Scene in Bukidnon
Location: Purok 3, Barangay Alae, Manolo Fortich, Bukidnon
Description: A 68-year-old man (alias 'Tino') was found dead with multiple stab wounds in Purok 3, Barangay Alae, Manolo Fortich, Bukidnon on 25 March 2026. Responding officers recovered a bladed weapon believed to have been used in the attack, along with two firearms: one .38 caliber revolver at the crime scene, and a second .38 caliber revolver inside a sling bag among the suspect's personal belongings left at the site. The primary suspect — alias 'Mando' — fled the scene and remains at large. Investigators attributed the killing to a land dispute; the suspect had allegedly threatened the victim earlier over a land management conflict. Hot pursuit operations were launched.
Weapons:
- 2 caliber .38 revolvers
- 5 rounds of ammunition
Actors:
Criminal Individual(s) (alias 'Mando,' suspect at large)
Police (Manolo Fortich MPS, Bukidnon)

The search warrant was issued by Executive Judge Rowena Nieves Tan of Pasay City Regional Trial Court Branch 118. - 3 rounds of ammunition

Title: Rido-Linked Groups Surrender 17 Firearms and Weapons in Maguindanao del Sur
Location: Mamasapano, Maguindanao del Sur
Description: Residents from 15 barangays in Mamasapano, Maguindanao del Sur surrendered 17 firearms to the 601st Infantry Brigade during a ceremonial turnover on 26 March 2026. The weapons were voluntarily surrendered by two formerly rival armed groups engaged in clan feuds (rido) from the municipality, signaling their intent to end the cycle of violence. The handover was facilitated by Lt. Col. Edgardo Batinay (34th IB) and Lt. Col. Erwin Felongco (38th IB), in coordination with Mamasapano Mayor Akmad 'Butch' Ampatuan Jr. and barangay officials. The event was linked to the Small Arms and Light Weapons (SALW) Management Program jointly implemented by OPAPRU and the 6th ID.
Weapons:
- 3 M16A1 assault rifles
- 4 M14 rifles
- 2 M1 Carbine rifles
- 2 craft rifles
- 2 RPG-2-style craft launchers
- 1 M203 grenade launcher
- 1 M79 grenade launcher
- 2 craft mortars
Actors:
Civilian Community (15 barangays of Mamasapano)
Communal/Political Militia (rival clans)
Military (34th Infantry Battalion; 38th Infantry Battalion; 601st Infantry Brigade, 6th Infantry Division/JTF Central)
Police (PNP, Mamasapano MPS)
Civilian Official(s) (Mayor Akmad 'Butch' Ampatuan Jr.; barangay officials)

Title: Police Remove WWII-era Unexploded Ordnance in Antipolo City
Location: Antipolo City, Rizal
Description: A citizen reported the discovery of an old, buried munition in Antipolo City on 27 March 2026. PNP Explosives and Ordnance Disposal/K9 personnel responded and safely conducted a render-safe procedure before removing the device. Authorities identified the item as a large WWII-era bomb that remained hazardous despite long burial. The ordnance was taken into expert custody for proper disposal.
Weapons: - 1 WWII-era munition
Actors:
Unknown/Unaffiliated Entity (concerned citizen who reported the discovery)
Police (PNP EOD/K9 Unit)

Title: Police Seize Pistol During Drug Buy-Bust Operation in Bukidnon
Location: Purok 3, Barangay Dalwangan, Malaybalay City, Bukidnon
Description: Police seized one caliber .45 pistol with three rounds of ammunition when they arrested a 23-year-old suspect (alias 'Nelson') and a 15-year-old minor accomplice during a buy-bust operation in Purok 3, Barangay Dalwangan, Malaybalay City, Bukidnon on 27 March 2026. The suspects attempted to flee but were arrested. An undercover officer purchased ₱500 worth of suspected shabu; a subsequent body search yielded seven additional sachets (~10g, estimated value ₱68,000), one sachet of suspected dried marijuana (~5g, ₱600), and buy-bust money. Title: Police Seize Pistol from Suspected DI-linked Drug Dealers in Maguindanao del Sur
Location: Sitio Gawad Kalinga, Barangay Poblacion, Datu Paglas, Maguindanao del Sur
Description:
PDEA-BARMM agents and police seized one caliber .45 pistol and ammunition along with five grams of suspected shabu (estimated value ₱35,000) when they arrested three suspects in a buy-bust operation in Sitio Gawad Kalinga, Barangay Poblacion, Datu Paglas, Maguindanao del Sur on 27 March 2026. Intelligence officials confirmed that all three suspects — Allan Manalasal, Tohamie Benito (alias 'Freddie'), and Basadurin Japal (alias 'Basco') — had links to remnants of the Dawlah Islamiya (DI) terror group, to whom they allegedly remitted drug proceeds in exchange for protection.

According to PhilStar, nearly half (30 of 68) of suspected shabu and cannabis dealers arrested by the authorities in BARMM since 2022 are linked to DI or BIFF. The arrest took place with the assistance of local executives, Muslim religious leaders, and barangay officials; local residents and their community leaders first reported the suspects' illegal activities to the authorities, enabling the operation.
Weapons:
- 1 caliber .45 pistol
- 19 rounds of ammunition
Actors:
NSAG (Dawlah Islamiya remnants, recipients of drug proceeds)
Criminal Organization (drug dealers)
Police (PDEA-BARMM; Datu Paglas MPS; Maguindanao del Sur Provincial Drug Enforcement Unit; PRO-BARMM)
Military (6th Infantry Division intelligence units)

Title: Former NPA Members Surrender Firearms and Explosives to Police in Ifugao
Location: Poblacion, Tinoc, Ifugao
Description: Dozens of former NPA members — alias "Juan" and 88 others — surrendered one 5.56mm rifle, one caliber .38 revolver, two rifle grenades, and 27 rounds of ammunition to the authorities in Poblacion, Tinoc, Ifugao on 27 March 2026. The surrender was facilitated by the 11th Special Action Battalion of the Special Action Force (SAF), in coordination with the 1501st Maneuver Company of the Regional Mobile Force Battalion 15, other PNP units, the Philippine Army, and the National Intelligence Coordinating Agency. The surrendering individuals are undergoing documentation and processing for possible enrollment in the Enhanced Comprehensive Local Integration Program (E-CLIP) to support their reintegration into civilian life.

- 2 M76 rifle grenades
- 27 rounds of ammunition
Actors:

Police (11th Special Action Battalion, SAF; 1501st Maneuver Company, Regional Mobile Force Battalion 15; other PNP units)

Title: Troops Recover NPA Arms Cache with Grenade Launchers in Bukidnon
Location: Barangays Kipaypayon and Mahayag, Quezon, Bukidnon
Description: Troops of the 89th Infantry (Makatao) Battalion recovered a suspected NPA cache containing firearms, improvised explosive devices, and other war matériel during an operation in Barangays Kipaypayon and Mahayag, Quezon, Bukidnon on 27 March 2026. Recovered weapons included two M79 grenade launchers, two KG9 submachine guns, four craft shotguns, one caliber .45 pistol, four caliber .357 revolvers, one caliber .38 revolver, one caliber .38 pistol-type firearm, and one caliber .22 pistol. Also seized were two improvised explosive devices (IEDs), one hand grenade, two unserviceable Baofeng radios, subversive documents, assorted ammunition, and various firearm parts and components. The battalion credited the success of the operation to timely information provided by local residents.

- 2 craft submachine guns
- 4 craft shotguns

Title: Police Seize Grenade from Barangay Councilman Over Alleged Shooting in Cebu
Location: Barangay Luca, Balamban, Cebu
Description:
Police seized one caliber .45 pistol, four rounds of ammunition, and one hand grenade when they served a search warrant on barangay councilman Carlo Ripdos Maunes, 39 (alias 'Kokong'), at his residence in Barangay Luca, Balamban, Cebu on 28 March 2026. The official was identified by two previously arrested suspects as the alleged mastermind of the 19 February 2026 shooting of fellow councilman Renato Tribunalo. Maunes was detained at the Balamban Municipal Police Station pending inquest proceedings. EOD personnel handled the grenade. The firearm was sent for ballistic testing to determine if it was used in the February shooting.

The two shooting suspects, Robert Borres Alcantara and Roger Markit Doncillo, claimed that Maunes paid them PHP30,000 to wound Tribunalo. Alcantara, the shooter, claimed the gun used in the shooting was supplied by Maunes and returned to him after. Alcantara and Doncillo claimed that Maunes had only paid them PHP2,000.
Weapons:
- 1 caliber .45 pistol
- 4 rounds of ammunition
- 1 Mk2 hand grenade
Actors:
Political Affiliate (barangay councilman)
Criminal Individual(s) (hired gunmen)
Police (Balamban Municipal Police Station; PNP EOD)

Title: MNLF Fighters Display Weapons at Founding Commemoration Event in North Cotabato
Location: Barangay Malabuaya, Kabacan, North Cotabato
Description:
Dozens of MNLF members displayed their weapons at a ceremony commemorating the 58th founding anniversary of the armed group in Barangay Malabuaya, Kabacan, North Cotabato on 28 March 2026. Hundreds of MNLF members attended the event, which was led by MNLF Acting Chairman, Central Committee Deputy Speaker Member of Parliament Abdulkarim Tan Misuari and MNLF Vice President for Political Affairs MP Datu Romeo Sema. Local government officials and law enforcement personnel also attended the event.
Images of the ceremony showed fighters armed with one M60 light machine gun, M4-pattern assault rifles, M16A1 assault rifles, M14 rifles, and M203 grenade launchers.
Weapons: - Dozens of weapons, including 1 M60 light machine gun, M4-pattern assault rifles, M16A1 assault rifles, M14 rifles, and M203 grenade launchers
Actors: NSAG (MNLF)

Title: Troops Seize M16 Rifle from NPA Militant After Clash in Occidental Mindoro
Location: Sitio Salafay, Barangay Monte Claro, San Jose, Occidental Mindoro
Description:
Troops of the 68th Infantry Battalion seized one M16 rifle, one hand grenade, and IED components and captured one female combatant following a 20-minute firefight with approximately 15 NPA fighters in Sitio Salafay, Barangay Monteclaro, San Jose, Occidental Mindoro on 29 March 2026. One officer — First Lieutenant Dean Buen Oyando, a Scout Ranger — was killed during the fight, and two soldiers were wounded. The clash occurred after another armed encounter in the same locale on 24 March (see PHL.2026.03.24.002).

The NPA's Southern Tagalog command (Melito Glor Command) issued a counter-statement claiming the firefight was a defensive action against troops of the 68th IB. NPA statements dispute the characterization of the captured female civilian as a rebel, identifying her as Charlize Garzon, claiming she is a cultural activist and AB Arts Management graduate.
Weapons:
- 1 M16A1 assault rifle
- 1 hand grenade
Actors:
NSAG (CPP-NPA, Lucio de Guzman Command, Melito Glor Command / Southern Tagalog Regional Operational Command)
Military (68th Infantry 'Kaagapay' Battalion; 203rd Infantry Brigade, 2nd Infantry Division)
Police (San Jose Municipal Police Station)

Title: Grenade Attack Over Wage Dispute Wounds Child in Cagayan de Oro City
Location: Sitio KM8, Barangay Lumbia, Cagayan de Oro City
Description: Two suspects — Arnold Bacarro, 20, a laborer, and his uncle Noel Bacarro — attacked the home of construction foreman Allan Albecio/Abecio with a shotgun and a grenade in Sitio KM8, Barangay Lumbia, Cagayan de Oro City on 29 March 2026. The suspects initially arrived demanding contact information for a contractor who allegedly failed to pay Arnold's wages; when the foreman could not provide it, Noel Bacarro allegedly fired a shotgun, forcing Albecio to take cover. The suspects left, then returned approximately 30 minutes later and lobbed a grenade into the compound, which detonated and wounded an eight-year-old boy in the leg/foot. The child was transported to Northern Mindanao Medical Center. The suspects fled by motorcycle; a manhunt was launched. Noel Bacarro is the subject of an existing arrest warrant.
Weapons:
- 1 shotgun (fired)
- 1 hand grenade (detonated)
Actors:
Criminal Individual(s) (Arnold Bacarro/Pat; Noel Bacarro/Pat)
Civilian Community (child victim; foreman Allan Albecio/Abecio)
Police (Cagayan de Oro City Police Office, Police Station 8, Lumbia)

Title: Authorities Recover NPA Arms Cache with Grenades and Explosives in Cagayan
Location: Barangay Minanga, Rizal, Cagayan
Description:
Police and Philippine Army personnel recovered an NPA arms cache in Barangay Minanga, Rizal, Cagayan on 29 March 2026, acting on intelligence provided by a former member. Recovered items included one caliber .45 pistol with one magazine, one caliber .38 revolver, one fragmentation hand grenade, three rifle grenades, five magazines for 5.56mm rifles, 119 rounds of assorted ammunition, one improvised explosive device (IED), and associated IED paraphernalia. Additional materials included commercial time fuse, detonating cord, commercial dynamite, blasting caps, an electric cap with leg wire, diesel fuel, and personal belongings.

The operation was led by the 1st Cagayan Provincial Mobile Force Company (1st CPMFC), with tactical support from the 11th Special Action Battalion (11SAB) of the SAF, other PNP units, and the Philippine Army. Recovered explosives were turned over to the Cagayan Provincial Explosive and Canine Unit (PECU), while other items were placed in Rizal Municipal Police Station custody.
Weapons:
- 1 caliber .45 pistol
- 1 caliber .38 revolver
- 1 Mk2 hand grenade
- 3 M76 rifle grenades
- 119 rounds of ammunition
- 1 IED and associated components
- commercial explosives and detonators
Actors:
NSAG (CPP-NPA)
Police (1st Cagayan Provincial Mobile Force Company; 11th Special Action Battalion, SAF; Rizal Municipal Police Station; Cagayan Provincial Explosive and Canine Unit)
Military (Philippine Army)

The Silongan group came under fire while traveling to meet a witness in a probe into the recent death of a Malaysian national in the area. Clearing operations were launched at approximately 4:45 p.m. to defuse the situation and recover weapons. Hot pursuit operations against those involved remain ongoing.

Barangay: Barangay Tubig Jatih and Tabuh Manok and Bulangsih, Panamao; Barangay Saimbangon, Pata, Sulu

On 8 April, troops from the 21st Infantry Battalion received one 40mm M79 grenade launcher and one Armscor .38 caliber revolver in Barangay Tubig Jatih, Panamao. On 9 April, two additional firearms were surrendered in Panamao—one shotgun in Barangay Tabuh Manok and one .45 caliber pistol in Barangay Bulangsih—facilitated through the initiative of local barangay officials. On 11 April, troops from the 104th Infantry Battalion received one M1 Garand rifle in Barangay Saimbangon, Pata, surrendered by the barangay chairman.

Title: Tapul Residents Surrender Fifteen Firearms in Sulu Amnesty Campaign
Location: Kalang Covered Court, Barangay Kalang, Tapul, Sulu
Description: Residents of Tapul, Sulu surrendered 15 firearms at the Kalang Covered Court on 8 April 2026. The RFGFPCC declaration was formalized through a municipal resolution read by Vice Mayor Nasser Daud Sr. and was the result of sustained collaboration among the Local Government Unit of Tapul, the 104th Infantry Battalion under the 1102nd Infantry Brigade of the 11th Infantry Division, and the Sulu Police Provincial Office. Key participants included Mayor Nasser Daud Jr., barangay leaders, religious figures, and members of the academe. The public presentation of the surrendered firearms was the ceremonial centerpiece of the event. Images showed three revolvers, three pistols, two M203 grenade launchers, one M16A1 assault rifle, and six M1 Garand rifles.

Title: Robbery Suspects Arrested with Improvised Firearm in Quezon City
Location: Susano Road, Barangay Sta. Monica, Novaliches, Quezon City, Metro Manila
Barangay: Susano Road, Barangay Sta. Monica, Novaliches

Later at approximately 14:30, aliases "Ronnel" and "Rodel" robbed a victim at gunpoint in Barangay Sta. Monica, stealing a cellphone; a subsequent follow-up operation led to their arrest on a nearby street, and police recovered the weapon. Three of the four suspects—Rex, Ronnel, and Rodel—have prior robbery records.
Weapons:
- 1 craft grenade launcher
- 1 40mm projected grenade

The operation was triggered by reports from T'boli tribal leaders about Narvasa's group extorting money and rice from villagers.

Title: Police Seize Revolver Following Child Abuse Incident in Quezon City
Location: Barangay Gulod, Novaliches, Quezon City, Metro Manila
Description: Police seized one .38 caliber revolver and five rounds of ammunition when they arrested a 46-year-old former security guard at his residence in Barangay Gulod, Novaliches, Quezon City on 9 April 2026 after he physically assaulted a 15-year-old female minor and threatened her with a firearm. Following a domestic altercation with his spouse, the intoxicated suspect directed his aggression toward the minor, kicking and punching her and threatening to kill her by pointing a revolver at her before she was rescued by a family member. QCPD Novaliches Police Station 4 officers, alerted via the Unified 911 emergency system, arrested the suspect after a brief foot chase. The minor was placed under her mother's care. The suspect faces charges for child abuse (RA 7610), illegal possession of firearms and ammunition (RA 10591), and resistance to authority (Article 151, RPC).
Weapons:
- 1 .38 caliber revolver
- 5 rounds of ammunition
Actors:
Criminal Individual(s) (unnamed 46-year-old former security guard)
Police (QCPD Novaliches Police Station 4)

Location: Barangay Banglay, Lagonglong, Misamis Oriental

Title: Police Seize Pistol from Robbery Suspect Following Shooting in Antipolo City

Description: Police seized one .45 caliber Armscor pistol (serial no. 1963275) and seven rounds of ammunition when they arrested alias "Ogag," 30, at a checkpoint in Barangay Sta. Cruz, Antipolo City, Rizal Province on 11 April 2026. Sources indicate that earlier the same day, Ogag and an unidentified companion had robbed a 47-year-old BPO employee along Sumulong Highway, shooting him in the left arm when he resisted. Ogag was subsequently flagged down during the checkpoint operation while riding a motorcycle matching the vehicle used in the robbery; he attempted to flee and resisted arrest before being subdued. His accomplice escaped. Ogag was charged with attempted robbery with frustrated homicide as well as violations of RA 10591, RA 10054 (Motorcycle Helmet Act), and RA 4136 (driving without a license).

Title: Troops Raid DI Arms Factory in Lanao del Sur
Location: Barangay Pabrica, Marogong, Lanao del Sur
Description: Troops of the 64th Infantry (Knights) Battalion seized a cache of war materials and firearms fabrication equipment formerly used by the Daulah Islamiya-Maute Group (DI-MG) when they raided a clandestine arms manufacturing site in Barangay Pabrica, Marogong, Lanao del Sur on 11 April 2026. The mission targeted a site identified through validated intelligence as a former DI-MG stronghold. No firefight occurred; the group had fled before troops arrived. Recovered unserviceable fabrication equipment included a milling machine, two lathe machines, a drill press, a welding machine, a power hacksaw, an anvil, and a jack stand. War materials seized included 50 pieces of .50 caliber tube barrels, one .50 caliber propellant cartridge, five Barrett suppressors, three Barrett magazines, twenty wooden frames for Barrett rifles, four burners for .50 caliber, two Barrett scopes, one 60mm tube, and one grenade. Military officials stated the recovery significantly degrades the DI-MG's capacity to rearm.
Weapons:
- 1 60mm mortar tube
- 1 grenade
- fabrication equipment (milling machine, 2 lathe machines, drill press, welding machine, power hacksaw, anvil, jack stand)
- assorted firearm parts and components
- 1 aerial bomb casing
Actors:
Military (PA, 64th Infantry Battalion, 101st Brigade, 1st Infantry Division)
NSAG (DI/Dawlah Islamiya-Maute Group)

Title: Amai Manabilang Residents Surrender 33 Weapons in Lanao del Sur
Location: Municipal Legislative Building, Amai Manabilang, Lanao del Sur
Description:
Residents of Amai Manabilang, Lanao del Sur surrendered firearms as part of a SALW Management Program turnover on 14 April 2026. The turnover was facilitated by troops of the 55th Infantry (Vigilant) Battalion in partnership with the LGU of Amai Manabilang and witnessed by Mayor Saidamem Leo Manabilang, Vice Mayor James Manabilang, and representatives from OPAPRU. The surrendered weapons — comprising Garand rifles, carbines, shotguns, and submachine guns, some bearing defaced serial numbers — were placed under the 55th IB's custody pending disposition in coordination with the Provincial SALW Valuation Committee; as part of the program, firearms were exchanged for livelihood packages.

Title: Government Forces Seize Weapons from High-Profile DI-MG Cell in Lanao del Sur
Location: Barangay Banga-Pantar, Marantao, Lanao del Sur
Description:
Government forces seized two M16 assault rifles, one M4 assault rifle, one M1 Garand rifle, one .38 caliber revolver, one F1 hand grenade, and IED components when they killed ten members of the Dawlah Islamiya-Maute Group (DI-MG) in Barangay Banga-Pantar, Marantao, Lanao del Sur on 17 April 2026. CIDG operatives and troops of the 103rd Infantry Brigade were serving multiple arrest warrants against DI-MG members for charges of murder, frustrated homicide, homicide, kidnapping, and serious illegal detention when the suspects opened fire. No casualties were reported on the government side; a wounded infant was rescued from the encounter site and immediately given medical attention.

Among the ten killed were the group's Amir, Amerol Mangoranca alias "Usman," and his sub-leader, Benjie Amir alias "Muslih/Benjie" — both listed in the AFP's Periodic Status Report for Q1 2026 and the primary subjects of the arrest warrants. The casualties included four women, including the wives of the militants. The group was directly linked to the 23 January 2026 ambush in Barangay Lininding, Munai, Lanao del Norte that killed four soldiers and wounded another, as well as Mangoranca's alleged role in planning the December 2023 bombing inside the Mindanao State University in Marawi that killed four Catholic worshippers. Two of the fatalities — Bato and Aragon — were former violent extremists who had previously surrendered to authorities before returning to armed activity.
Weapons:
- 2 M16A1 assault rifles
- 1 M4 assault rifle
- 1 M1 Garand rifle
- 1 .38 caliber revolver
- 1 F1 hand grenade
- ~150 rounds of ammunition
Actors:
Military (PA, 103rd Infantry Brigade, 1st Infantry Division, JTF-Zampelan, Western Mindanao Command)
Police (CIDG-BAR, CIDG Cotabato City Field Unit, PNP Special Action Force, PRO-BAR, Lanao del Sur PPO, NICA-10)

Title: Troops Seize 24 Firearms Following Clashes with NPA that Left 19 Dead in Negros Occidental
Location: Sitio Sinugmawan and Sitio Plariding, Barangay Salamanca, Toboso, Negros Occidental
Barangay: Sitio Sinugmawan and Sitio Plariding, Barangay Salamanca
Description:
Troops of the 79th Infantry (Masaligan) Battalion seized 24 firearms — including seven M16 rifles, three M14 rifles, three M1 Garand rifles, one M1 carbine rifle, one M203 grenade launcher, six .45 caliber pistols, and three .357 Magnum revolvers — along with unspecified explosives when they killed 19 NPA in a series of firefights in Barangay Salamanca, Toboso, Negros Occidental on 19 April 2026. The operation began in Sitio Sinugmawan after troops, acting on a civilian tip, moved in on the rebels' position. As the insurgents scattered, blocking forces engaged them in a running gun battle at Sitio Plariding that lasted several hours. One soldier was wounded. 3rd Infantry Division Commander Major General Michael Samson described the battle as the highest death toll in a single encounter in Negros since the 1990s.

Among those killed was long-wanted NNF leader Roger Fabillar (aliases: Arnel Tapang, Jhong, Nono, Domeng), commander of the NNF Special Operations Group and Centro de Gravedad, who carried a PHP 2-million bounty and was linked by the military to the summary execution of at least 36 civilians tagged as informants, as well as the killing of Police Master Sgt. Aguilar in Calatrava. The fighting displaced 168 families (approximately 653 individuals) from Barangays Salamanca and San Jose to nearby schools; over 200 residents in adjacent Escalante City also evacuated as a precaution.

Following the encounter, the UP Diliman University Student Council identified USC Education and Research Councilor Alyssa Alano — who had been conducting a community immersion among farmers in the area — as among the 19 fatalities, condemning her death as the killing of an innocent civilian. The UP Office of the Student Regent issued a separate statement expressing condemnation.
Weapons:
- 9 M16A1 assault rifles
- 3 M14 rifles
- 3 M1 Garand rifles
- 1 Type 56-2 assault rifle
- 1 M1 carbine rifle
- 1 M203 grenade launcher
- 2 .45 caliber pistols
- 1 .357 Magnum revolvers
- at least 2 M76 rifle grenades
- at least 2 40mm projected grenades
- ~500 rounds of ammunition
Actors:
Military (PA, 79th Infantry Battalion, 303rd Infantry Brigade, 3rd Infantry Division)
NSAG (CPP-NPA, Northern Negros Front/KR-N)

The surrender was facilitated by the agency's secretary, together with a family member of the late owner, who sought police assistance for the proper disposition of the weapons, most of which had expired firearm registrations.

All items were placed under secure custody at Balanga City Police Station pending further verification with the Firearms and Explosives Office; some units required additional validation due to documentation concerns, including instances of identical serial numbers.

Title: Police Seize Pistol, Grenade from Robbery Suspects in Caloocan City

Description: Police seized a 9mm pistol, one grenade, and ammunition when they arrested six individuals along A. Mabini Street, Barangay Maypajo, Caloocan City on 8 May 2026. The incident took place following reports of a failed robbery attempt by a criminal group on the residence of a vlogger. The suspects were aboard a black Toyota Innova bearing conduction sticker No. Y5G662 when intercepted. During the operation, a dismissed Police Staff Sergeant identified as "JR," formerly assigned to the NCRPO until his dismissal in 2023 for grave misconduct, drew a firearm while attempting to flee and was shot in the foot by responding operatives; he was taken to a hospital under police custody. Police also seized suspected shabu. The suspects included a traffic enforcer, a driver, a hairdresser, a pizza vendor, the dismissed officer, and a sixth male suspect. The group had been monitored for about a month prior to the operation and was linked to robbery schemes including "rent-tangay," "gapos gang," and "cake for mayor" methods.
Weapons:
- 1 9mm pistol
- ~8 rounds of ammunition
- 1 M26 IM hand grenade (still in its transport packaging)
Actors:
Former Police/Soldier (alias JR, dismissed Police Staff Sergeant — robbery suspect, shot in foot)
Criminal Organization (5 additional robbery suspects)
Police (PNP-HPG SOD, Task Force Limbas, Northern Police District, RHPU NCR Intel, Caloocan City Police Station, PRO 4A 1st PMFC, Batangas PPO)
