Residents from various barangays in South Upi, Maguindanao del Sur surrendered 48 loose firearms — described as comprising assault rifles, pistols, grenade launchers, bolt-action sniper rifles, and other small arms — to the 57th Infantry Battalion at the Municipal Gymnasium of Barangay Romongaob on 14 May 2026. The ceremony marked the official launch of the SALW Management Program at the LGU level. Beneficiaries immediately received livelihood assistance — including cows, goats, fertilizers, corn seeds, and sacks of rice — in exchange for surrendering their weapons.

Images of the event showed two different displays of weapons — one display appeared to have more large-caliber craft rifles and grenade launchers than the other. The incident inventory is based on the display with more complete photographic evidence (i.e. the one with fewer craft rifles and grenade launchers), though the difference between the two displays cannot be accounted for.
Weapons:
- 1 M16A1 assault rifle
- 1 M14 rifle
- 3 M1 Garand rifles
- 8 M1 carbine rifles
- 5 craft shotguns
- 6 craft rifles
- 1 M79 grenade launcher
- 1 craft grenade launcher
- 5 craft submachine guns
- 8 pistols
- 8 revolvers
- 1 craft handgun
Actors:
Civilian Community (surrenderers, South Upi barangays)
Military (PA, 57th Infantry Battalion, 603rd Infantry Brigade, 6th Infantry Division, Western Mindanao Command)
Police (Maguindanao del Sur PPO, PCOL Michael John Mangahis)
Civilian Official(s) (Mayor Helen A. Padua, South Upi; Presidential Assistant David B. Diciano, OPAPRU; Dr. Aliuddin Haron, MILF Joint Normalization Committee)

Title: Troops Seize Firearms After Five NPA Rebels Killed in Negros Occidental
Location: Barangays Abaca and Poblacion, Cauayan, Negros Occidental
Description:
Troops of the 15th Infantry Battalion seized eight firearms and killed five CPP-NPA members identified as remnants of the South-West Front, Komiteng Rehiyon Negros (KRN) in a series of armed encounters in Barangays Abaca and Poblacion, Cauayan, Negros Occidental on 16 May 2026. The operation followed a community tip about the presence of armed rebels in the area. The seized weapons included one M60 light machine gun, M16 assault rifles, an M14 rifle, an M203 grenade launcher, and a hand grenade.

The five killed were identified as Rolando Dantes Jr., Jobert Casipong, Gilbert Tingson, Alex Languita, and Francis Vince Dingding alias Poy — described as a rebel leader and former activist previously affiliated with the UP Cebu community. Sources noted the band was responsible for multiple civilian killings in southern Negros Occidental since 2025. The Cauayan local government subsequently initiated steps to declare the CPP-NPA persona non grata in the municipality.
Weapons:
- 1 M60 light machine gun
- 1 M203 grenade launcher
- 1 M14 rifle
- 4 M16 assault rifles (presumed)
- 1 hand grenade
Actors:
NSAG (CPP-NPA, South-West Front KRN — Rolando Dantes Jr., Jobert Casipong, Gilbert Tingson, Alex Languita, Francis Vince Dingding alias Poy — all killed)
Military (PA, 15th Infantry Battalion, 302nd Infantry Brigade, 3rd Infantry Division)
Civilian Official(s) (Cauayan LGU)

Title: Authorities Seize Rifles from Former Barangay Chairman in Maguindanao del Norte
Location: Barangay Piers, Buldon, Maguindanao del Norte
Description:
Authorities seized two M16 assault rifles, a grenade launcher, and ammunition during a warrant operation in Barangay Piers, Buldon, Maguindanao del Norte on 18 May 2026, in which former Barangay Chairman Odin Abdul Ayunan and his 13-year-old grandson, Saidamin "Bibitok" Mahid, were killed. The target of the operation was Ayunan, listed as the No. 2 Municipal Most Wanted Person in Buldon and facing arson charges.

According to CIDG, Ayunan and his son allegedly resisted arrest and fired at operatives, prompting a firefight during which both Ayunan and his grandson were killed. The CIDG stated the recovered firearms and grenade launcher were found during the clearing operation. However, Ayunan's daughter, Saada Odin, publicly disputed the account, alleging that her father and brother did not resist, that operatives entered without properly presenting the warrant, and that the incident escalated without cause. She also raised questions about how a 13-year-old could have handled firearms. Local officials coordinated with village and municipal authorities to prevent retaliatory violence following the operation. The family described the broader context as a long-running dispute dating to March 2024.
Weapons:
- 2 M16 assault rifles
- 1 M203 grenade launcher
- ~100 rounds of ammunition
Actors:
Political Affiliate (Odin Abdul Ayunan — former barangay chairman)
Military (PA, Marine Battalion Landing Team 2; RMFB 14-A)
Police (CIDG Cotabato City FU; CIDG RSOT; CIDG Maguindanao PFU; RIU 15-CIT Cotabato; Buldon MPS)

Title: Zamboanga del Sur Residents Surrender 95 Firearms and 64 Explosives to Authorities
Location: Pavilion Hall, Provincial Government Complex, Barangay Dao, Pagadian City, Zamboanga del Sur
Barangay: Pavilion Hall, Provincial Government Complex, Barangay Dao
Description:
Residents from various municipalities of Zamboanga del Sur surrendered 95 firearms, 64 explosives, and 132 rounds of ammunition to the 53rd Infantry Battalion at the Provincial Government Complex in Barangay Dao, Pagadian City on 19 May 2026. The event marked the province's fifth anniversary as an insurgency-free province and was attended by Presidential Peace Adviser Secretary Mel Senen Sarmiento and various provincial, military, and law enforcement officials.

Surrendered weapons included M14 rifles, M16 assault rifles, one M653 assault rifle, M1 Garand rifles, carbines, M79 grenade launchers, shotguns, KG9 submachine guns, and various pistols and revolvers. Explosives turned over included 40mm projected grenades, RPG munitions, hand grenades, rifle grenades, and ammunition. The event also featured the awarding of 31 scholarships to beneficiaries under the second batch of the SALW Management Program, and the ceremonial distribution of PAMANA Livelihood Program checks to community cooperatives.

- 1 M653 assault rifle

- ~47 pistols and revolvers

- 132 rounds of ammunition

Title: 108 Firearms Surrendered under SALW Management Program in Lanao del Sur
Location: Taraka Municipal Gymnasium, Taraka, Lanao del Sur
Description:
Residents of Tamparan, Lumba Bayabao, Mulondo, and Taraka in Lanao del Sur surrendered 108 firearms to military and government authorities at a SALW Management Program event at the Taraka Municipal Gymnasium on 19 May 2026. The event was led by OPAPRU Secretary Mel Senen Sarmiento, and attended by military, provincial, and municipal leaders. Brig. Gen. Dela Rosa noted that 588 loose firearms had been turned over to the 103rd Infantry Brigade under the SALW program.

The surrendered cache included sniper and assault rifles, rocket-propelled grenade launchers, grenade launchers, heavy and sub-machine guns, carbines, shotguns, and pistols ranging from .50 to 9mm caliber. The SALW Program is a key security component of the Annex on Normalization under the Comprehensive Agreement on the Bangsamoro (CAB). The turnover was jointly implemented by the Provincial Government of Lanao del Sur, the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM), the local governments of the participating municipalities, the AFP, and the PNP.

The victim was shot by a 16-year-old gunman who, when the pistol failed to fire again after the victim fell, struck her head with the pistol grip. The three suspects — alias Tinoy (18, lookout) and two 16-year-old minors (including the gunman) — were arrested in a hot pursuit operation by police. Investigation revealed the attack was a paid killing arranged by an alleged drug-related mastermind, alias Manoy (at large), who offered the group PHP 5,000 cash and three "bulto" of shabu. The motive was reportedly that the victim owed 100 grams of shabu (valued at PHP 680,000) to Manoy. Police said the case was drug-related and the gunman was a two-time alumnus of Operation Second Chance with a prior criminal record.

Title: PNP Revokes Sen. Dela Rosa's Firearms License and Orders Surrender of 117 Guns
Location: Camp Crame, Quezon City, Metro Manila
Description:
The Philippine National Police Firearms and Explosives Office (PNP-FEO) revoked the License to Own and Possess Firearms (LTOPF) and firearms registrations of Senator Ronald "Bato" dela Rosa — encompassing 117 assorted firearms registered under his name — at Camp Crame, Quezon City on 26 May 2026, ordering their immediate seizure in response to a pending criminal case before the International Criminal Court (ICC).

Dela Rosa's registered collection, described in detail by the National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) in its 25 May 2026 letter to PNP chief Gen. Jose Melencio Nartatez Jr., is substantial and varied. The NBI characterized the arsenal as including semi-automatic and select-fire rifles, UZI submachine gun variants, high-powered .338 caliber rifles, battlefield-grade shotguns, and a broad array of handguns. Dela Rosa held a Type 5 LTOPF — a classification permitting an individual to own fifteen or more firearms and designating the holder as a licensed firearms collector — which was valid until 10 March 2034. Sources differ slightly on the total count: the PNP-FEO revocation order and multiple outlets cite 117 registered firearms, while the NBI's letter and several news reports reference 118; the discrepancy appears to stem from whether a single firearm was counted in both agencies' records. All registered firearms were listed at Dela Rosa's address in Davao City, Davao del Sur.

The revocation was the latest escalation in a broader constitutional and political crisis surrounding Dela Rosa's status as an ICC fugitive. Dela Rosa, who served as the first PNP Chief under former President Rodrigo Duterte from 2016 to 2018, was named co-perpetrator alongside Duterte in ICC charges of crimes against humanity of murder in connection with the administration's anti-drug campaign, which human rights groups estimate killed up to 30,000 people. The ICC issued a confidential arrest warrant against Dela Rosa in November 2025; the warrant was unsealed upon his unexpected return to the Senate on 11 May 2026 after a six-month absence. The Senate placed him under protective custody, but Dela Rosa escaped through the Senate building on 14 May following a shooting incident in the chamber that prompted confusion and a brief lockdown. The Supreme Court denied his petition for a temporary restraining order on 20 May, and the Department of Justice subsequently directed the PNP and NBI to enforce the ICC warrant. The NBI publicly designated Dela Rosa "armed and dangerous" given the scale of his personal arsenal, a characterization disputed by his legal team. As of 28 May 2026, neither Dela Rosa nor his camp had surrendered any of the 117 firearms; PNP spokesperson Col. Allen Rae Co stated that any police operation to retrieve the weapons was an "operational matter" that could not be prematurely disclosed and that there were "no deadlines" for compliance.

Timeline of key events: In November 2025, the ICC issued a confidential arrest warrant against Dela Rosa for his alleged role in facilitating former President Rodrigo Duterte's violent drug war. On 11 May 2026, the warrant was unsealed upon his unexpected return to the Senate after a six-month absence. On 21 May, the DOJ ordered the PNP and NBI to act on the ICC warrant. On 25 May, NBI Director Melvin Matibag sent a formal letter to the PNP chief requesting the immediate cancellation of Dela Rosa's firearms licences and the surrender or confiscation of his 118 registered firearms within 48 hours, citing officer safety concerns. On 26 May, FEO chief Brig. Gen. Jojo Manalad signed the revocation order citing Section 4(g) of RA 10591; RCSU 11 received the order electronically the same day. On 27 May, RCSU 11 formally served the revocation order at Dela Rosa's registered Davao City address; it was received by his legal representative Atty. Vicente Adrian Pajaro. Also on 27 May, joint CIDG-NBI teams raided a house in Diamond Subdivision, Barangay Balibago, Angeles City, Pampanga believed linked to Dela Rosa, finding neither the senator nor his firearms. On 28 May, Dela Rosa's counsel Atty. Israelito Torreon publicly challenged the revocation's legal basis, arguing the PNP-FEO misapplied Section 4(g) — which governs licensing eligibility rather than revocation — instead of Section 39; he further contended that the ICC does not constitute a "court of law" under Philippine statute. The PNP defended its position, stating the licensing conditions constitute a continuing requirement and the revocation was "immediately executory."
Weapons: - 117 (or 118) assorted registered firearms, comprising semi-automatic rifles, assault rifles, UZI submachine gun variants, high-powered .338 caliber rifles, shotguns, and handguns. No individual weapon was seized or surrendered during this incident
Actors:
Political Affiliate (Sen. Ronald "Bato" dela Rosa — subject of the revocation order; firearms holder)
Police (PNP Firearms and Explosives Office; PNP Regional Civil Security Unit 11; PRO 11; Davao City Police Office; PNP CIDG NCR)
Other State Agency (National Bureau of Investigation — initiating the revocation request)

Title: Police Seize Assault Rifle and Pistol from Cigarette Smugglers in Davao del Norte
Location: Barangay Lapaz, Carmen, Davao del Norte
Description:
Law enforcement personnel seized a caliber .40 Glock 23 Gen 5 pistol, a CMMG Banshee 300 MK4 AR-pattern assault rifle, and ammunition when they intercepted a jungkong vessel and land convoy transporting approximately 900 master cases of smuggled cigarettes valued at PHP 22–22.5 million at a riverside area in Barangay Lapaz, Carmen, Davao del Norte on 4 June 2026. Authorities arrested eight individuals.

The operation was led by CIDG Regional Field Unit 11, Carmen Municipal Police Station, and Highway Patrol Group Davao del Norte, with support from the Regional Special Operations Group 11, Davao Norte PNP intelligence units, 2nd Davao del Norte Mobile Force Company, Philippine Coast Guard, and JTF Sinaya. Authorities identified the cigarettes as originating from Jolo, Sulu, lacking customs documentation and tax stamps. The PNP chief ordered a deeper investigation into possible inter-regional smuggling networks across Mindanao.
Weapons:
- 1 caliber .40 Glock 23 Gen 5 pistol
- 1 CMMG Banshee 300 KM4 assault rifle
- 128 rounds of 9mm ammunition
- 60 rounds of 5.56mm ammunition
Actors:
Criminal Organization (smuggling network, 8 individuals)

Title: Troops Seize Assault Rifles, M14 From DI Militants in Maguindanao del Sur
Location: Barangay Pimbalakan and Barangay Daladap, Mamasapano, Maguindanao del Sur
Description: Troops of the 38th Infantry Battalion seized one M16A1 assault rifle, one M14 rifle, and one Remington R4 assault rifle in two separate engagements with Dawlah Islamiya-Hassan Group (DI-HG) militants in Mamasapano, Maguindanao del Sur on 5 June 2026. In the first encounter, troops engaged an undetermined number of DI-HG members in Barangay Pimbalakan; the group, which is believed to have been involved in a deadly ambush of police officers in March 2026, retreated and abandoned the M16A1 and M14 rifles. In a second encounter in Barangay Daladap, troops engaged another DI-HG element, recovering the Remington R4 rifle believed to have belonged to a soldier of the 40th Infantry Battalion killed in a March 2024 ambush. Philstar reported that four DI-HG members were wounded across the two clashes.
Weapons:
- 1 M16A1 assault rifle

- 2 rounds of 5.56mm ammunition
Actors:
NSAG (Dawlah Islamiya-Hassan Group)

Title: Wanted Robbery Suspect Arrested with Revolver and Hand Grenade in Taguig City
Location: Barangay West Rembo, Taguig City, Metro Manila
Description: Police arrested a suspect and seized one caliber .38 revolver, three rounds of ammunition, and one hand grenade concealed in a shoulder bag at an impounding compound in Barangay West Rembo, Taguig City on 6 June 2026. The suspect was observed openly handling a firearm in a suspicious manner; he failed to produce the required licensing documents and was immediately arrested. Title: Civilian Surrenders FN FAL Rifle in Sulu
Location: Barangay Hambilan, Pandami, Sulu
Description: A civilian surrendered one FN-FAL assault rifle to the 104th Infantry Battalion in Barangay Hambilan, Pandami, Sulu on 7 June 2026 through the RFGFPCC Campaign. The firearm was formally turned over by Benhur A. Sitin, Secretary of the Sangguniang Bayan of Pandami, who stated it had been placed under the custody of the Municipal Mayor after the anonymous owner voluntarily surrendered it. The surrender was also attributed to the swift government response to an indiscriminate firing incident involving alias "Jiry" on 5 June 2026. Since January 2026, the 1102nd Infantry Brigade had recovered a cumulative 177 firearms across the second district of Sulu through the RFGFPCC Campaign.
Weapons: - 1 FN FAL assault rifle
Actors:
Military (PA, 104th Infantry "Para sa Bayan" Battalion, Alpha Company; 2nd Civil-Military Operations Company; 15th Civil-Military Operations Battalion; 1102nd Infantry "Ganarul" Brigade)
Police (Pandami Municipal Police Station)

Title: Police Seize Pistol From Murder Suspect in Bulacan
Location: Barangay Muzon, San Jose del Monte City, Bulacan
Description: Police seized a pistol from a murder suspect after they shot and killed a 37-year-old TNVS (Grab) driver in a dispute over the vehicle locks in Barangay 176-A, Caloocan City on 7 June 2026. Dashcam footage captured the two conversing before the passenger drew a firearm and fired several shots, then fled. In a follow-up operation, police arrested the suspect — a 43-year-old male — at his residence in Barangay Muzon, San Jose del Monte City, Bulacan and recovered the firearm believed to have been used in the killing.
Weapons: - 1 pistol
Actors:
Criminal Individual(s) (murder suspect)
Police (Caloocan City Police Station; Northern Police District)

Title: Fugitive Former Police Officer Arrested with 9mm Pistol in Quezon City
Location: Barangay Pasong Tamo, Quezon City, Metro Manila
Description: Police seized one 9mm Taurus pistol when they arrested dismissed Police Lt. Col. Rafael Dumlao III at a safehouse in Barangay Pasong Tamo, Quezon City on 9 June 2026. Dumlao was convicted in 2024 of kidnapping and killing South Korean businessman Jee Ick-joo inside Camp Crame in 2016, and was a fugitive for two years. The pistol was registered to another individual. The arrest followed a three-week surveillance operation triggered by monitoring of family movements linked to Dumlao's daughter's wedding. Authorities seized his cellphone and laptop for forensic examination to identify individuals who may have assisted him in hiding.
Weapons: - 1 9mm Taurus pistol (registered to another individual)
Actors:
Former Police/Soldier (Rafael Dumlao III, dismissed PLTCOL)
Police (PNP-CIDG NCR)
